data.current_description: Sends a custom event to Heap. Rate limited to 30 requests per 30 seconds per identity per app_id.
data.description_suggestion: Sends a custom event to Heap for analytics tracking. Accepts event name, user identification, timestamp, and custom properties. Rate limited to 30 requests per 30 seconds per identity per app_id. Returns 200 on successful ingestion. Events are asynchronously processed and made available in Heap analytics.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on what inputs are expected (event name, properties, user identification) and what the response indicates. Added details about expected inputs, rate limiting context, and side effects (asynchronous processing) to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'identifyUser' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the linking action. Added details about required inputs, the purpose of identification, and how it affects subsequent event attribution for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Links an anonymous user_id to a known identity string.
data.summary_suggestion: Link anonymous user to known identity
data.description_suggestion: Associates an anonymous user_id with a known identity string (email, username, or custom identifier). Requires app_id and both user_id and identity parameters. This operation enables user recognition across sessions and devices. Returns 200 on successful identification. Subsequent events from this user_id will be attributed to the identified user.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks context about required parameters, what constitutes a valid identity, and the side effects of identification. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the linking action. Added details about required inputs, the purpose of identification, and how it affects subsequent event attribution for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Attaches custom properties to an account, supporting both single and bulk updates.

data.current_description: Sends multiple events in a single request for third-party integrations.
data.description_suggestion: Sends multiple events in a single request, optimized for third-party integrations and high-volume event ingestion. Accepts an array of event objects, each containing event name, user identification, timestamp, and custom properties. Rate limits are applied per batch. Returns 200 on successful ingestion. All events in the batch are processed asynchronously and made available in Heap analytics.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about what constitutes the request format, how many events can be included, and rate limiting behavior for bulk operations. Added clarity on expected input structure (array of event objects), rate limiting context for bulk operations, and processing behavior to help AI agents understand when to use bulk vs. single event tracking.

data.current_description: Generates an authentication token for user deletion operations. Requires Basic Auth with app_id and api_key.
data.description_suggestion: Generates an authentication token required for user deletion operations. Requires Basic Auth credentials with app_id as username and api_key as password. Returns an authentication token with an expiration time. The token must be included in subsequent user deletion requests. Tokens are single-use or have limited validity; obtain a new token for each deletion operation.
